workflow.name: Enrich Google Sheets rows via any REST API in rate-limited batches
workflow.description:
## How it works
• Reads all rows from a Google Sheet
• Splits them into configurable batches (default: 5 rows per batch)
• Calls any external REST API once per row (GET or POST, Header Auth)
• Waits 1 second between batches to stay within API rate limits
• Routes responses: writes enriched data back to the sheet on success, logs errors on failure
• Loops through all batches until the full sheet is processed
• Want more REST API workflows? See [n8n webhook & API templates ranked by demand](https://www.telosignal.com//explorer/webhook-api)

## Set up steps
• Takes ~10 minutes to configure
• Connect your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential to both Sheet nodes
• Set your Sheet ID and tab name in the Read Rows node
• Paste your API endpoint URL in the Call API node and connect your Header Auth credential
• Adjust batch size and wait duration via the sticky notes inside the workflow
• Update the Update Row node with the column(s) you want to write back

parameters.content:
## Enrich Google Sheets via API — Rate-Limited Batches

This template reads rows from a Google Sheet, calls any external API to enrich each row, and writes results back, in rate-limited batches to avoid hitting API rate limits.

## Who it's for
Developers, marketers, and operations teams who need to enrich spreadsheet data (leads, products, URLs) via any REST API without triggering rate-limit errors.

## How it works
1. **Read rows** from your input Google Sheet tab
2. **Split into batches** (default: 5 rows per batch)
3. **Call your API** for each row in the batch
4. **Wait 1 second** between batches (rate-limit pause)
5. **Check the response** route success and errors separately
6. **Write enriched fields** back to the matching row on success
7. **Capture errors** with row identifier on failure

## Requirements
- Google Sheets OAuth2 credential
- HTTP Header Auth credential (for your API key)

## How to set up
1. Connect your **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential to both Sheet nodes
2. Set your **Sheet ID** and **Tab name** in the Read Rows node
3. Set your **API endpoint URL** in the Call API node
4. Connect your **Header Auth** credential in the Call API node
5. Update the **Update Row** node with the column(s) to write back
6. Adjust **batch size** and **wait duration** using the sticky notes below

## Batch Size & Rate Limit Config

**SplitInBatches node** (`Batch Rows`)
→ Open the node and set `Batch Size`
→ Default: **5 rows per batch**
→ Increase for fast APIs (e.g. 20)
→ Decrease for strict rate limits (e.g. 1)

**Wait node** (`Rate Limit Pause`)
→ Default: **1 second** between batches
→ Change `Amount` to match your API's rate limit
→ Example: 2s for 30 req/min APIs

## API Credential Setup

**Call API node** (`Call API`)
1. Open the node
2. Set `URL` to your API endpoint
   Example: `https://api.example.com/enrich`
3. Set `Method` (GET or POST)
4. Under **Authentication**, select `Header Auth`
5. Create a new **Header Auth** credential:
   - Name: `X-API-Key` (or whatever header your API requires)
   - Value: your API key

**Google Sheets nodes**
- Both Sheet nodes need the same **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential
- Grant access to the spreadsheet in Google Drive

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
